title: Try a query-ranking model where each race-lap is a group
summary: Pit-next-lap is partly a within-lap ranking problem: among drivers on the same race/lap, who is most likely to box now?
rationale: Pit-next-lap is partly a within-lap ranking problem: among drivers on the same race/lap, who is most likely to box now? CatBoost supports ranking objectives with `GroupId`, including pairwise/query losses, and keeps strong native handling of high-cardinality categoricals.
implementation_hint: Create groups like `Race_Year` + `LapNumber` or `Race_Year` + coarse stint window. Train `CatBoostRanker` with `QueryCrossEntropy` or `PairLogitPairwise`; convert scores to probabilities later with OOF calibration. Use raw categoricals instead of manual one-hot so CatBoost can form CTR/combo features.
expected_effect: Could improve ordering for AUC without needing perfect absolute calibration, especially when multiple cars have similar tyre age but only one is strategically exposed.
risk: Ranking groups may be too small/noisy on some laps; if positives are extremely sparse within groups, benefit may vanish or training may become unstable.
